 {"id":"bd-2qd","title":"Define per-extension scenario suite for capture","description":"Background:
- Each extension may expose tools, slash commands, and event hooks; we must test them consistently.

Steps:
- For each sampled extension, enumerate available features (tools/commands/events).
- Define minimal smoke scenarios + edge cases (inputs, expected outputs).
- Document any required configuration or secrets (mocked).

Output:
- A scenario specification mapped to each extension in the sample manifest.

Acceptance:
- Every extension has at least one scenario per supported feature category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:28.650419393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619339695Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619279633Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extension capture scenario suite spec","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qd","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:34:39.934693450Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qk","title":"Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity","description":"# Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature/functionality coverage of the original Pi Agent (TypeScript) with:
- Complete conformance harness validating behavioral equivalence
- Comprehensive test coverage without mocks where possible
- Full integration with sibling libraries (asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust)
- Detailed E2E integration test scripts with verbose logging

## Background
pi_agent_rust is a Rust port of Pi Agent (TypeScript CLI) by Mario Zechner. The port uses
spec-first methodology: extract behavior from legacy → implement from spec → never translate
line-by-line. Currently at ~85% feature complete with all core functionality working.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-02)
- ✅ Core Types: All message types, content blocks, usage tracking
- ✅ 7 Built-in Tools: read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls (122 conformance tests)
- ✅ Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure (all streaming + tool use)
- ✅ Agent Runtime: Full loop with tool iteration and event callbacks
- ✅ Session Management: JSONL v3 format, tree structure, branching
- ✅ Interactive TUI: Elm Architecture with bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour
- ✅ CLI: Full argument parsing, subcommands, package management
- ✅ Authentication: auth.json storage, OAuth login/logout
- ✅ Compaction: Full context compaction with LLM summarization
- ✅ RPC Mode: Complete JSON protocol implementation
- ✅ Benchmarks: Truncation, SSE parsing with performance budgets

## Key Gaps Requiring Work
1. **HTTP Migration**: Providers still use reqwest; asupersync HTTP client stubbed
2. **Extensions Runtime**: Protocol defined but no WASM loader/runtime
3. **Session Index Integration**: Implemented but not wired into CLI
4. **Themes**: Not implemented
5. **Test Coverage**: Critical gaps in provider streaming, TUI, CLI, compaction

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All providers use asupersync HTTP client (tokio removed)
- [ ] Extension runtime loads and executes WASM extensions
- [ ] Session picker uses SQLite index for fast lookups
- [ ] Theme system loads and applies themes
- [ ] 95%+ test coverage without mocks for core paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real API calls (recorded/VCR style)
- [ ] All conformance fixtures pass
- [ ] Performance targets met (startup <100ms, binary <20MB)

## Workstreams (child features)
1. HTTP Migration (HIGH priority) - Blocks tokio removal
2. Test Coverage Expansion (HIGH priority) - Quality gate
3. Session Index Integration (MEDIUM priority) - UX improvement
4. Themes (LOW priority) - Nice to have
5. Extensions Runtime (tracked separately in existing beads bd-25q)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29.025096826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cass + EXTENSIONS.md notes for port completeness:
- Extension runtime is explicitly Node/Bun-free: Tier A WASM component (default), Tier B JS compiled to QuickJS bytecode or JS→WASM, Tier C MCP process IPC.
- Connector model: pi.tool/exec/fs/http/session/ui/events hostcalls only; capability-gated, auditable, no ambient OS access.
- QuickJS event loop must drive microtasks + hostcall futures deterministically (asupersync LabRuntime-friendly).
- Protocol is versioned JSON + WIT; extension_ui_request/response + cancelable hooks are first-class and must be covered by conformance + benchmarks.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:21Z"},{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added missing Phase-9-ish beads not previously represented in open/in-progress work:
- Release engineering workstream: bd-gqtd (with versioning/changelog/CI-matrix/GH releases/docs/badges subtasks)
- Error UX workstream: bd-3am2 (hint mapping + rendering + tests)
- Rustdoc workstream: bd-14od (public API audit + doc build check)
- Troubleshooting doc: bd-3oa9 (under docs parity bd-3m7f)

Per guidance, I did not create/claim new extension-runtime work; these are non-extension polish/release/documentation gaps.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:25:25Z"},{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added additional non-extension gap-tracking beads (docs/spec drift + AgentCx + property tests):

- bd-gor5 (workstream) Docs/spec drift fix (README/PLAN/FEATURE_PARITY)
  - bd-30zd README asupersync/tokio status correction
  - bd-jw5q PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md reconcile checklist → beads
  - bd-33jg FEATURE_PARITY.md refresh statuses + correct bead refs (themes/conformance)
  - bd-24rp AGENTS.md remove stale tokio-migration statements

- bd-3i7u asupersync: define AgentCx capability wrapper (child of bd-20c)

- bd-2lr3 (workstream) Property-based + fuzz testing for core parsers (child of bd-26s)
  - bd-30hr proptest truncation invariants
  - bd-2sl9 proptest SSE parser chunk-boundary robustness
  - bd-2127 proptest session JSONL roundtrip + tree invariants

Intent: eliminate doc drift, make the remaining live plan fully represented in Beads, and add extra safety nets (property tests) beyond legacy parity.","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qrp","title":"Settings: UI toggles (quietStartup, collapseChangelog, hideThinkingBlock, cursor, padding)","description":"# Goal
Expose common UI/display settings in `/settings` and ensure they take effect.

# Settings (legacy docs/settings.md)
- `quietStartup` (hide startup header)
- `collapseChangelog` (condense changelog)
- `hideThinkingBlock`
- `showHardwareCursor`
- `doubleEscapeAction` (tree|fork)
- `editorPaddingX`
- `autocompleteMaxVisible`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggles can be changed via `/settings`.
- [ ] Changes persist.
- [ ] Interactive UI responds immediately where feasible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:56.637633490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:22.665988486Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-1cd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:22.665953010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-2mcr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:22.071456803Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:57.011492648Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"Connector: Filesystem minimal API (capability-scoped)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **tight, minimal filesystem connector** that supplies just enough for extensions without Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Minimal API: read, write, list, stat, mkdir, delete (if required).
- Path allowlist + canonicalization; no path traversal.
- Symlink policy: either disallow or resolve to allowlist roots.
- Explicit capability scopes (read/write per path prefix).
- Structured logs for each FS operation (path hash + scope).

# Security Invariants
- Every FS call checks capability scope before side effects.
- All paths are normalized and validated.

# Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist enforcement + symlink escapes.
- E2E security suite attempts traversal with log verification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:30.145210806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:56:25.476115727Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:56:25.476050806Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:55.332471251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:55.792528568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2skp","title":"Workstream: Message Queue Parity (steering vs follow-up)","description":"# Goal
Implement **legacy Pi message queue semantics** in interactive mode, including steering vs follow-up delivery, queue UI, and keybinding integration.

# Background (Legacy Spec)
Legacy pi-mono allows submitting messages while the agent is still working:

From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Message Queue”:
- **Enter** queues a *steering* message, delivered after current tool execution (interrupts remaining tools)
- **Alt+Enter** queues a *follow-up* message, delivered only after the agent finishes all work
- **Escape** aborts and restores queued messages to editor
- **Alt+Up** retrieves queued messages back to editor

Settings (legacy `docs/settings.md`):
- `steeringMode` and `followUpMode` can be `"one-at-a-time"` (default) or `"all"`.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/config.rs` already has `steering_mode` / `follow_up_mode`, but interactive does not implement the queue.
- Interactive key handling is hard-coded; Enter submits only when idle.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Queue data model
- Represent queued items as:
  - kind: steering | follow_up
  - text
  - timestamp/sequence
- Store in PiApp state; ensure deterministic ordering.

## 2) Delivery points (core semantics)
- **Steering**: deliver after the current tool finishes (or next safe checkpoint) and cancel remaining tool iterations for the current user request.
- **Follow-up**: deliver only once the agent reaches an “idle” boundary (no more tools, assistant finished).

## 3) Modes: one-at-a-time vs all
- `one-at-a-time`:
  - deliver at most one queued message per boundary, wait for response before sending next.
- `all`:
  - flush all queued messages at the boundary (preserving order), then process.

## 4) Keybinding behavior
- Enter while busy → enqueue steering
- Alt+Enter while busy → enqueue follow-up
- Escape while busy → abort + restore all queued to editor
- Alt+Up → move queued back into editor for editing

(These should be expressed as actions and wired via the keybindings workstream.)

## 5) UI
- Render queued messages in a dedicated area (legacy has a pending messages container).
- Clear, minimal indicators:
  - show count by type
  - show the actual queued text (truncated) so the user knows what will be sent

# Testing
- Deterministic state machine tests for:
  - enqueue ordering
  - delivery boundaries
  - one-at-a-time vs all
  - abort + restore

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue behavior matches legacy semantics.
- [ ] Modes are configurable via settings.
- [ ] UI makes queued messages visible and understandable.

# Dependencies
- Depends on keybinding actions (`bd-3ip`) for correct shortcut routing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.354917446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.361927478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2skp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.361889558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sl9","title":"Proptest: SSE parser robustness (chunking + boundaries)","description":"## Goal
Property-test the SSE parser so arbitrary chunk boundaries and adversarial inputs don’t break parsing invariants.

## Why
SSE parsing is extremely sensitive to chunk boundaries. Real networks can split at any byte.

## Invariants
- Parsing is chunk-boundary invariant: splitting the same byte stream into random chunks yields identical parsed events.
- Parser never panics.
- Parser either produces valid events or a well-typed error (no silent corruption).
- For known-valid SSE inputs, the parser produces the expected event sequence.

## Acceptance
- New proptest module exists for `src/sse.rs`.
- CI runtime stays bounded.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.300424890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.309184171Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sl9","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.309152653Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
